scenario.name: empty
scenario.description: No watchlist, no positions. Baseline: only market-level obs should fire.

calls.claim: Short intraday positions in Real Estate and Utilities sectors while maintaining long exposure to Energy sector.
calls.warrant: The sector rotation data shows Energy outperforming by +1.45% while Real Estate and Utilities underperform by -1.93% and -1.75% respectively, indicating strong intraday momentum divergence that typically persists through the trading session.
calls.qualifier: Intraday only; unwind all positions before market close; size each short position at ≤1.5% of book.
calls.rebuttal: If Energy sector reverses to negative territory while Real Estate and Utilities turn positive before 2 PM ET.

calls.claim: Reduce overall market exposure by 15-20% and increase cash allocation.
calls.warrant: Only 25 of 98 S&P100 stocks advancing indicates severe breadth weakness, which historically precedes broader market declines as narrow leadership fails to sustain rallies.
calls.qualifier: Execute if S&P 500 breaks below its 50-day moving average; limit reduction to 20% maximum of equity portfolio.
calls.rebuttal: If market breadth improves to 60+ S&P100 stocks advancing by tomorrow's close.

call_001.critique: Highly actionable with specific sectors, clear warrant, precise qualifiers, and concrete rebuttal trigger.

call_002.critique: Clear portfolio action with specific percentage, warrant explains mechanism, qualifier has threshold, rebuttal is observable.

l3_summary.description: No watchlist, no positions. Baseline: only market-level obs should fire.

scenario.name: thin
scenario.description: One watchlist symbol, one position. Minimum viable state.

calls.claim: Implement tactical short-term rotation from lagging sectors to leading sectors.
calls.warrant: Sector rotation combined with weak market breadth suggests capital is moving between sectors rather than entering the market broadly, creating opportunities in relative sector performance despite overall market weakness.
calls.qualifier: Rotate 5-10% of portfolio from bottom 3 performing sectors to top 3 performing sectors daily; unwind positions if sector rotation momentum index drops below 0.2.
calls.rebuttal: If sector rotation momentum becomes indiscriminate with all sectors declining together.

calls.claim: Overweight Energy sector relative to Real Estate in sector rotation trades.
calls.warrant: Energy sector leading with +1.45% while Real Estate lags with -1.93% demonstrates clear sector rotation momentum that can be exploited through relative strength positioning.
calls.qualifier: Enter pairs trade with 3:1 ratio favoring Energy over Real Estate ETFs; exit when Energy/Real Estate relative performance ratio crosses below its 10-day moving average.
calls.rebuttal: If Energy sector underperforms Real Estate by more than 2% on any single trading day.

calls.claim: Hedge or reduce AAPL exposure ahead of earnings report.
calls.warrant: AAPL faces earnings risk with its report in 8 days, creating uncertainty that typically increases volatility and downside risk in the pre-earnings period.
calls.qualifier: Buy 30-day ATM puts on AAPL covering 50% of position; implement when implied volatility reaches 25% above 30-day average.
calls.rebuttal: If AAPL pre-earnings implied volatility remains below 20% (indicating low market concern).

call_001.critique: Clear action with specific thresholds; grounds directly support rotation strategy.

call_002.critique: Specific pairs trade with measurable exit condition; grounds directly justify sector overweight.

call_003.critique: Concrete hedging action with precise volatility trigger; grounds directly address earnings risk.

l3_summary.description: One watchlist symbol, one position. Minimum viable state.

calls.claim: Buy XOM calls or stock for a bounce from oversold levels ahead of earnings
calls.warrant: XOM is at the 8th percentile of its 20-day range, indicating extreme oversold conditions that often precede a technical bounce. The elevated ATM IV of 44.5% suggests options are pricing in significant earnings volatility, which could work in favor of long positions if earnings beat expectations or guidance is positive.
calls.qualifier: Enter only if XOM remains below the 15th percentile of its 20-day range; size at ≤ 3% of book; unwind within 5 trading days (before or after earnings)
calls.rebuttal: If XOM breaks below its 52-week low before earnings

calls.claim: Buy Energy sector ETF (e.g., XLE) calls while selling Real Estate sector ETF (e.g., XLRE) calls to capitalize on sector rotation
calls.warrant: Energy sector is leading with +1.45% while Real Estate is lagging with -1.93%, showing clear intraday rotation. Weak market breadth (only 25 of 98 S&P100 stocks advancing) suggests defensive rotation into sectors like Energy that may benefit from inflation concerns, while rate-sensitive sectors like Real Estate underperform.
calls.qualifier: Execute as a pairs trade with 1:1 notional exposure; unwind if Energy sector relative strength vs Real Estate drops below the 5-day moving average
calls.rebuttal: If Energy sector underperforms Real Estate by >1% on the next trading day

calls.claim: Buy XOM puts ahead of earnings due to elevated implied volatility and weak market conditions
calls.warrant: XOM's ATM IV of 44.5% indicates options are pricing in significant earnings risk. Combined with weak market breadth (only 25 of 98 S&P100 stocks advancing), there is elevated risk of a negative earnings reaction even if results are in-line, as weak macro conditions could amplify any disappointment.
calls.qualifier: Buy 30-45 DTE ATM puts; size at ≤ 1.5% of book; exit if IV drops below 35% before earnings
calls.rebuttal: If WTI crude oil prices rise >5% before XOM earnings

call_001.critique: Clear buy action on XOM with specific entry, sizing, and exit conditions; grounds directly support bounce thesis; warrant explains oversold bounce and IV dynamics; qualifier has thresholds; rebuttal is concrete falsifier.

call_002.critique: Explicit pairs trade with ETFs; grounds support rotation thesis; warrant links rotation to macro weakness; qualifier defines unwind condition; rebuttal has specific performance threshold.

call_003.critique: Direct buy puts action on XOM; grounds combine earnings risk and macro weakness; warrant explains IV and macro amplification; qualifier specifies DTE, sizing, IV exit; rebuttal uses concrete oil price move.

calls.claim: Trim NVDA and UNH positions as they approach extreme overbought levels near earnings.
calls.warrant: NVDA at 94th percentile and UNH at 100th percentile of their 20-day ranges indicate stretched valuations, and GOOGL/MSFT near range tops with earnings in 7 days suggests potential for profit-taking or volatility spikes that could affect sentiment broadly.
calls.qualifier: Reduce position size by ≤ 1% of book per stock if they trade above 95th percentile of 20-day range.
calls.rebuttal: If NVDA and UNH both break above their 20-day highs with volume > 150% of average, signaling continued momentum.

calls.claim: Buy XOM as a contrarian play given oversold conditions in a leading sector.
calls.warrant: XOM is near-bottom at the 8th percentile of its 20-day range, indicating oversold conditions, while the Energy sector leads with +1.45%, showing relative strength and rotation into the sector.
calls.qualifier: Enter if XOM closes below the 10th percentile of its 20-day range; size at ≤ 3% of book.
calls.rebuttal: If Energy sector reverses to decline > 2% intraday, negating rotation thesis.

calls.claim: Sell or hedge TSLA positions ahead of earnings to reduce volatility exposure.
calls.warrant: TSLA reports earnings in 0 days, and the theme shows that earnings events create significant implied volatility (GOOGL's ATM IV at 42.5% implies 2.7% daily moves). This suggests high near-term price uncertainty, making risk management prudent.
calls.qualifier: Execute before market close today; use ATM puts with ≤ 30-day expiry, not weeklies.
calls.rebuttal: If TSLA's pre-earnings IV drops below 30% before close, indicating reduced market anxiety.

call_001.critique: Clear action with thresholds, grounds directly support, warrant explains mechanism, specific qualifier, concrete falsifier.

call_002.critique: Explicit buy action, grounds align perfectly, warrant connects oversold to rotation, specific entry condition, observable sector reversal.

call_003.critique: Concrete sell/hedge instruction, grounds directly support, warrant explains volatility risk, specific timing/instrument, measurable IV threshold.

scenario.name: iv_heavy
scenario.description: Symbols with upcoming earnings, testing IV signal density.

calls.claim: Reduce exposure to META, GOOGL, and MSFT ahead of earnings due to elevated positioning risk at technical extremes.
calls.warrant: Stocks trading near 52-week highs (88th-100th percentile of 20-day range) face asymmetric downside risk into earnings, as positive expectations are already priced in while negative surprises can trigger sharp mean reversion, especially given META's historical avg |move| of 9.3% and GOOGL's ATM IV of 42.5% indicating elevated uncertainty.
calls.qualifier: Trim positions to ≤ 1.5% of book per name; execute within 3 trading days before earnings.
calls.rebuttal: If S&P 500 advances > 1.5% on the day before earnings, indicating broad market strength that could override individual stock technicals.

calls.claim: Buy 30-day ATM puts on GOOGL as a tactical hedge against earnings volatility.
calls.warrant: GOOGL's ATM IV at 42.5% is elevated relative to typical pre-earnings levels, creating expensive but justified option premiums given the binary earnings outcome; buying puts provides asymmetric payoff if earnings disappoint, with IV crush post-earnings limiting time decay if earnings are positive.
calls.qualifier: Size at ≤ 0.5% of book; target 30-day expiration, not weeklies.
calls.rebuttal: If GOOGL's implied volatility term structure inverts (front-month IV < back-month IV) before earnings, indicating reduced near-term uncertainty.

calls.claim: Overweight Energy sector exposure relative to Real Estate and Utilities.
calls.warrant: Energy leading at +1.45% while Real Estate and Utilities lag at -1.93% and -1.75% shows capital rotation into cyclical/value sectors, which may persist if economic growth expectations improve or inflation concerns resurface; this rotation offers relative strength opportunities.
calls.qualifier: Add Energy exposure if XLE/XLU ratio crosses above its 10-day moving average; cap at 3% of book.
calls.rebuttal: If WTI crude oil drops > 5% in a single session, undermining Energy sector momentum.

call_001.critique: Clear action with specific targets, grounds directly support, warrant explains mechanism, qualifier has thresholds, rebuttal is concrete.

call_002.critique: Specific portfolio action, grounds support, warrant explains IV dynamics, qualifier has size/expiry limits, rebuttal is observable.

call_003.critique: Concrete sector rotation action, grounds directly support, warrant explains rotation logic, qualifier has trigger/threshold, rebuttal is specific.

l3_summary.description: Symbols with upcoming earnings, testing IV signal density.
